title: Legacy Day 12 to Current Day 13 Requirement Identity Map

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Every material assertion distinguishes subject, speaker, knowledge object type, supporting evidence, confirmation or contestation state, purpose, and permitted period of use.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: An assertion is classified as material when any persistence, sharing, decision, identity, downstream-use, or difficult-reversal criterion applies.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Implementations represent the minimum knowledge-status vocabulary defined by RFC-0001.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Uncertain materiality defaults to material until a role-appropriate review records a narrower classification.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Stored knowledge-status distinctions remain inspectable even when the user interface uses simpler labels.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A use is classified as high impact when it can cause irreversible, punitive, rights-restricting, sensitive-disclosure, institutional, or durable-profile consequences.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A high-impact contested assertion should not drive irreversible or punitive action while review is pending, except under a narrowly documented safety duty.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Every high-impact classification records responsible actor, basis, affected people, consequence, duration, review point, and appeal or correction route.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Parent-authored data must not be labeled as the child's confirmed profile.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Representative authority records type, subject, evidence, allowed and prohibited actions, validity, conflicts, notice, and independent-review route.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A model inference must not be hidden or represented as fact.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Age alone does not establish complete representative authority.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A disputed label must not be retained without its disputed state being visible.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A governed disclosure basis records purpose, authority, minimum necessity, recipient, duration, onward-use limits, notice or delay reason, constraints, reviewer, and appeal.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A temporary crisis state must not become a permanent identity.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Every material assertion distinguishes subject, speaker, knowledge-object type, evidence, uncertainty, confirmation or contestation, purpose, impact, visibility, active period, and dependent records.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Original source and revision history must not be removed from a material assertion.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A third-party statement, professional hypothesis, or model inference does not silently become a subject-confirmed fact.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Contestability must not be denied solely because the speaker is a professional.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A subject record contains identity, type, age or capacity band where relevant, relationship, participation state, visibility policy, representative references, jurisdiction profile, and review date.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Refusal to agree must not be treated as evidence that an assertion is correct.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A speaker record contains identity, type, role, relationship, authority scope, conflict status, and source time.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: One family member's private statement must not be exposed to another without a governed basis.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: An assertion record contains source, status, content, evidence, confidence, uncertainty, materiality, impact, validity, purpose, visibility, contestation, dependencies, lineage, and supersession fields.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A person reviewing an interpretation should be able to inspect source, speaker, AI involvement, evidence, confidence, active period, dispute state, correction route, and dependent decisions.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A contestation record contains requester authority, requested action, reason, constraints, conflict check, reviewer, decision, dependent-record actions, appeal, and resolution time.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Every material collection or use has a traceable basis containing subject, operator, purpose, data scope, access scope, processing or use, retention rule, review point, objection route, and applicable exception basis.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Every implementation declares a jurisdiction profile covering locations, asserted bases, age and representative rules, rights, retention, safeguarding duties, conflicts, owner, and review date.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Mandatory or emergency processing must not be misrepresented as freely given consent.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Absent, expired, or uncertain jurisdiction qualification does not support a universal lawfulness claim.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Rights-request support should include access, source explanation, correction, contextualization, restriction, objection, export, deletion, withdrawal, review of automated or high-impact interpretation, and appeal.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Every material collection or use has a traceable basis covering subject, operator, purpose, category, jurisdiction, data, access, use, retention, review, rights, authority, and exceptions.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Every retention rule states purpose, retained data, storage location, access, review point, deletion or archival trigger, backup handling, and restricted exceptions.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Future improvement, unknown future value, or mere data existence is not a sufficient purpose.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A retention exception is specific, minimally scoped, access-restricted, time- or review-bounded, explained where safe and lawful, and appealable or independently reviewable where appropriate.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Implementations distinguish optional permission, requested service, safety, mandatory, security, approved research or evaluation, and unknown or disputed basis categories.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: All MingOS channels should resolve to a governed identity, permission, memory, and audit layer.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A jurisdiction profile states whether and how each technical category maps to a lawful or professional basis.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A channel-specific local record must not become an untraceable shadow profile.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Mandatory, emergency, safety, or institutional processing is not represented as freely given consent.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: General early-access or contact forms should minimize fields, state purpose and retention, identify recipients and request routes, prohibit sensitive case submission without governed intake, avoid pre-checked optional permissions, and separate distinct purposes.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A purpose definition contains identity, description, affected people, data, operations, recipients, basis categories, jurisdiction, retention, risk, owner, and status.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Sensitive child cases must not be collected through a general marketing field without a governed basis.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A permission or processing-basis record contains person or representative, authority, purpose, category, jurisdiction, scope, version, time, review, withdrawal, constraints, exceptions, notice, and conflict status.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Private case data must not be reused for model training merely because it exists.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Representative authority records identity, subject, type, evidence, allowed and prohibited actions, conflicts, validity, notice, and independent review.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Data must not be scattered so broadly that correction or deletion becomes impossible.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Age or family role alone does not create unlimited representative authority.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Data must not be retained indefinitely for unspecified improvement.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Conflict, disputed authority, or high-impact rights requests route to a conflict-free reviewer.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A retention exception must not be hidden inside general terms.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A data asset inventory contains subjects, source, category, sensitivity, locations, purposes, roles, retention, deletion dependencies, backup, derivation, export, and audit references.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: An emergency or mandatory process must not be described as voluntary consent.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Rights-request support includes access, explanation, correction, contextualization, restriction, objection, export, deletion, withdrawal, high-impact review, and appeal.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Staff must not receive access broader than their current purpose requires.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A rights-request record contains requester, represented subject, authority evidence, conflict check, scope, verification, owner, status, located data, derived and backup locations, exceptions, decision, evidence, independent review, and appeal.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Visible deletion must not leave active copies silently retained elsewhere.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Every asset maps to a named retention profile.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Every high-impact safety action has a named responsible role, recorded basis, bounded action, handoff state, and review path.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A safety action is classified as high impact when it can restrict rights, disclose sensitive information, involve external authority, create durable risk labels, change service, or be difficult to reverse.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: AI must not become the final unaccountable actor in safety triage or action.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Every high-impact safety action records actual authority, evidence, uncertainty, severity, bounded action, proportionality, handoff or availability, review, and correction or appeal.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: The selected safety response should be the least intrusive action that reasonably addresses the identified risk.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A queue, model, mailbox, or unnamed team is not treated as a named responsible role.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: MingOS must not claim to provide emergency response, diagnosis, legal advice, child-protection authority, or guaranteed human response unless actually staffed and governed.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: P0 classification means credible imminent or unfolding severe harm where delay may materially increase danger.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Public and in-product messaging states the system role, AI or human status, response limits, route to local qualified support, and availability limits.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: P1 classification means credible serious risk requiring prompt accountable review without current evidence establishing immediate P0.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Non-immediate safety classifications should be contestable.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A safety classification records recency, report source, plan or means where relevant, protective factors, uncertainty, classifier, and next review.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Safety review preserves original evidence, model and policy version, responsible reviewer, classification changes, false-positive or false-negative outcomes, and affected downstream records.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Each service model defines response targets and escalation ownership without creating a universal emergency-response promise.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A disputed safety label must not become a permanent identity marker.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Every implementation publishes and records one current service profile: active handoff, bounded review, no handoff, or unverified.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: MingOS must not automatically side with an operator who may be causing harm.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A service profile records responsible role, hours, response target, locations, languages, unavailable services, failed-handoff action, verification, expiry, and owner.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: The pathway should support independent review, minimum disclosure, child or third-party protection, conflict handling, and documentation of access restrictions.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A no-handoff or unverified service does not mark handoff complete or imply active human monitoring.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Case and cross-family evidence remains purpose-limited, access-restricted, provenance-preserving, and resistant to re-identification.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Case and cross-family evidence remains purpose-limited, access-restricted, provenance-preserving, and resistant to re-identification.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Public repository records should contain method, limitations, aggregate findings, objections, and decisions.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Public repository records should contain method, limitations, aggregate findings, objections, and decisions.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Public records must not contain raw identifiable family or child material.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Public records must not contain raw identifiable family or child material.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Cross-family analysis states purpose, minimizes fields, documents inclusion and exclusion, preserves uncertainty and counterexamples, avoids ranking, restricts raw-case browsing, receives privacy and bias review, and expires or revalidates derived rules.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Cross-family analysis states purpose, minimizes fields, documents inclusion and exclusion, preserves uncertainty and counterexamples, avoids ranking, restricts raw-case browsing, receives privacy and bias review, and expires or revalidates derived rules.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: External model providers must not receive case data merely because the application uses their API.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: External model providers must not receive case data merely because the application uses their API.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Raw family cases must not be committed to the public repository.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Raw family cases must not be committed to the public repository.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Recognizable combinations must not be published as anonymous cases.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Recognizable combinations must not be published as anonymous cases.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Consultation data must not be reused for marketing.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Consultation data must not be reused for marketing.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Cross-family analysis must not create family scores or rankings.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Cross-family analysis must not create family scores or rankings.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Rare cases must not be used as model examples without separate review.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Rare cases must not be used as model examples without separate review.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Analysts must not access full cases when aggregates suffice.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Analysts must not access full cases when aggregates suffice.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Generated summaries must not be treated as de-identified by default.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Generated summaries must not be treated as de-identified by default.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Data must not be preserved indefinitely because future research may be useful.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Data must not be preserved indefinitely because future research may be useful.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Every material public claim is traceable to a repository source, evidence source, or explicit capability-status label.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Every material public claim is traceable to a repository source, evidence source, or explicit capability-status label.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Official public surfaces should use the defined capability-status vocabulary.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Official public surfaces should use the defined capability-status vocabulary.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Vision must not be worded as a delivered capability.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Vision must not be worded as a delivered capability.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A public Charter summary states that it is a summary, identifies the governing document, status, version, synchronization date, change history, and discrepancy-report route.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A public Charter summary states that it is a summary, identifies the governing document, status, version, synchronization date, change history, and discrepancy-report route.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: The website must not silently rewrite Candidate or Accepted commitments for marketing.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: The website must not silently rewrite Candidate or Accepted commitments for marketing.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Material architecture, Charter, product, and governance pages should display source ID, version or commit, status, review date, capability status where relevant, owner, and correction route.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Material architecture, Charter, product, and governance pages should display source ID, version or commit, status, review date, capability status where relevant, owner, and correction route.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A material claim record should preserve exact wording, audience, supporting source, limitations, reviewer, publication date, and expiry or re-review date.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A material claim record should preserve exact wording, audience, supporting source, limitations, reviewer, publication date, and expiry or re-review date.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Public forms state purpose, required and optional fields, recipient, retention, request route, human or automated follow-up, prohibited sensitive content, and relevant privacy notice.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Public forms state purpose, required and optional fields, recipient, retention, request route, human or automated follow-up, prohibited sensitive content, and relevant privacy notice.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A general early-access form should not solicit identifiable child, crisis, medical, or consultation details.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A general early-access form should not solicit identifiable child, crisis, medical, or consultation details.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: Official surfaces make visible AI involvement, fallibility, human-review status, available professional role, unavailable emergency or clinical services, and response-availability limits.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: Official surfaces make visible AI involvement, fallibility, human-review status, available professional role, unavailable emergency or clinical services, and response-availability limits.

legacy_mappings.legacy_statement: A future implementation should provide repository-to-site synchronization, a normative-page manifest, status/version checks, stale-content release controls, discrepancy reporting, and release notes.
legacy_mappings.current_statement: A future implementation should provide repository-to-site synchronization, a normative-page manifest, status/version checks, stale-content release controls, discrepancy reporting, and release notes.
